title: Ireland's First Offshore Wind Farm Reaches Financial Close
excerpt: A landmark moment for Ireland's renewable energy ambitions as the Codling Wind Park secures final investment approval, paving the way for construction to begin this summer.
content:
Ireland has reached a historic milestone in its clean energy transition as the Codling Wind Park, located off the coast of County Wicklow, has achieved financial close. The 1.3 GW project represents the single largest infrastructure investment in the history of the Irish State and is expected to power over one million homes when fully operational.

The project will create an estimated 2,500 construction jobs and 500 permanent operations and maintenance roles over its 30-year lifetime. Skillnet Ireland's Offshore Wind Academy (SOWA) has been working closely with the project developers to ensure a pipeline of skilled workers is available to meet demand.

"This is the moment we've been preparing for," said the Minister for the Environment, Climate and Communications. "The skills infrastructure we're building through initiatives like SOWA will ensure Irish workers benefit directly from this once-in-a-generation investment."

Construction is expected to begin in Q3 2026, with first power generation targeted for 2029.
author: SOWA Editorial Team

title: SOWA Launches National Skills Platform for Offshore Wind Careers
excerpt: The Skillnet Offshore Wind Academy unveils Ireland's first dedicated careers and skills platform for the offshore wind energy sector, connecting job seekers with training and career pathways.
content:
The Skillnet Offshore Wind Academy (SOWA) has officially launched Ireland's first national platform dedicated to careers in offshore wind energy. The platform brings together career pathway mapping, skills diagnostics, training directories, and labour market research in a single, accessible resource.

Developed in partnership with Wind Energy Ireland, the Department of Further and Higher Education, and leading industry employers, the platform addresses a critical gap identified in the 'Building our Potential' report — the need for a centralised resource to guide individuals into offshore wind careers.

Key features include an interactive career pathway map showing 12 key roles across 7 sectors, a skills diagnostic tool that assesses readiness and recommends personalised training, and a comprehensive directory of 15+ accredited training courses from Irish providers.

"We know the jobs are coming — 30,000 roles by 2030," said the SOWA Programme Director. "This platform ensures people can see themselves in those roles and find a clear path to get there."
author: SOWA Editorial Team

title: Cork Port Designated as Primary Offshore Wind Operations Hub
excerpt: The Port of Cork has been selected as a key operations and maintenance base for Ireland's southern offshore wind projects, creating hundreds of permanent skilled roles in the region.
content:
The Port of Cork has been officially designated as the primary operations and maintenance hub for offshore wind projects in the Celtic Sea, following a significant investment in port infrastructure upgrades.

The designation means that the port will serve as the home base for crew transfer vessels, maintenance teams, and logistics operations for multiple offshore wind farms currently in development. An estimated 400 permanent jobs will be based at the facility, spanning technicians, marine coordinators, logistics managers, and HSE professionals.

The announcement has been welcomed by local training providers, with the National Maritime College of Ireland and Munster Technological University both expanding their offshore wind training programmes in response to anticipated demand.

"Having a world-class port facility with co-located training providers gives Cork a unique advantage," said the Port of Cork CEO. "We're creating a cluster of excellence for offshore wind operations that will serve Ireland for decades."
author: SOWA Editorial Team

title: Two Free Skillnet-Funded OWE Courses Now Open for Enrolment
excerpt: SOWA announces two fully funded online courses — Introduction to Offshore Wind Energy and SCADA Data Analysis — available at no cost to eligible Irish workers and job seekers.
content:
The Skillnet Offshore Wind Academy is now accepting enrolments for two fully funded training courses designed to help Irish workers enter or upskill within the offshore wind energy sector.

The 'Introduction to Offshore Wind Energy' course is a two-week online programme covering the technology, policy landscape, career opportunities, and Ireland's project pipeline. It's designed for complete newcomers and requires no prior qualifications. The next cohort begins on 5 May 2026.

The 'SCADA Data Analysis for Wind Energy' course is a four-week online programme teaching participants to analyse turbine performance data using Python and Power BI. It's aimed at those with some technical or data background looking to specialise in wind energy analytics. The next cohort begins on 14 July 2026.

Both courses are fully funded by Skillnet Ireland and are available at no cost to participants. Places are limited and allocated on a first-come, first-served basis.

"Removing the cost barrier is essential for widening participation," said the SOWA Training Coordinator. "These courses are specifically designed to give people a foot in the door of this growing sector."
author: SOWA Editorial Team
